constraints: Read-only per task source and Agent tool policy; native_grant capabilities [read, web], write_effective=false.
Do not run provider-heavy checks; checker observations here are limited to file-shape reads, not executed --all or live-provider profiles.
Must not claim customer success/quality/source-truth/Movement authority.

Pre-P12 green expectations (carried as caller/COO gating, not asserted as proven here): checker gate `--all` exit 0 in-worktree; git diff --check clean; import smoke clean. AGENTS.md historical snapshot lists these; this plan does not execute them.

Customer-entry launch must declare project_ref OR explicit output_root: the ref-less DEFAULT_BUILDINGS_ROOT resolves under $BRICK_HOME/~/.brick (caller-local evidence home), NOT the repo-local project vessel (P7 evidence-root policy).
selected_adapter_ref is fail-closed (no support default); the work root in four-llm-standard-graph carries the only write NEED, so the caller must declare a narrow write_scope at intake.
report.env must be sourced for Slack/dashboard signals; Slack/dashboard real delivery is gated by env credentials plus allow_real_*_delivery flags, dry-run otherwise.
missing_scope: P12 customer success/quality verdict — owned by the human gate, never produced by this Building chain.

Live four-provider (Codex/Claude/Gemini-API/Fugu) execution-and-return under the full write graph: only a read-only single-pass smoke (four-llm-standard-graph-readonly-smoke-0625) and a deterministic Gemini-API fixture (P5) exist; live API credential validity and parallel fan-out are unproven.

An active-worktree-vs-frozen-repo discriminator surface for a cold customer session was not located by the gap2 readiness chain (recorded missing there); a customer cannot yet be told how to tell an active worktree from a frozen brick-protocol checkout.

Docs-vs-CLI entry-path consistency: a populated customer-entry readiness matrix and one reconciled cold-start first-read path (README/DEPLOY vs brick console + FIRST_USE.md) remain owed (gap2-0625 + 0624 kernel P7 delta).
An external fresh-machine/release-artifact dry-run transcript (clone -> install.sh -> brick init -> FIRST_USE.md -> brick verify) converting the source-confirmed entry chain into run evidence.

Open four-LLM disposition: the smoke flagged a fan-in strict-gate asymmetry (link-gate:strict on gemini/fugu fan-in edges but not the claude fan-in edge vs the preset's symmetric strict-evidence/fan-in-wait-all profile) and absence of raw/agent-return.jsonl + evidence/claim_trace for read-only smokes — both queued for Smith/COO, undecided.

Documentation count drift to reconcile before a customer reads it: setup.md and AGENTS.md state '13 profiles' under support/checkers/profiles/, but 24 profile YAMLs are currently present (observed by Glob).
P9 module-diet write-scope conflict map (core.yaml / module_registry.yaml / case_runners.py) is requested but the followup Building's authored plan was not confirmed produced in this read.
not_proven: Customer success, quality, or source truth for any P12 dogfood run — owned by the human gate.

This is a plan node only: it bounds scope and surfaces gaps; it performs no implementation, no edits, no provider-heavy runs, and makes no success/quality/Movement claim.
observed_evidence: Two public launch paths are documented and live: run_building_intake (preset path; task_statement verbatim -> work/task.md; selected_adapter_ref fail-closed) and assemble (new-structure builder DSL); run_composed_graph_intake is sealed from the public surface (support/docs/references/{quickstart.md,launch-guide.md}).

Customer-entry surface exists at content level: support/operator/first_use.py renders FIRST_USE.md, a 'brick' console entrypoint + brick verify + onboard doctor/wizard exist; the full chain (install.sh -> brick console -> cli _cmd_init -> first_use.py -> FIRST_USE.md -> brick verify) is mapped by status/kernel/gap2-customer-entry-readiness-0624.md (confirmed by the gap2-customer-entry-readiness-0625 review/closure nodes).

P4 transition: brick/templates/presets/four-llm-standard-graph.md now declares the gemini-broad-review inspect lane on adapter:gemini-api; report_sinks.py distinguishes Slack lane labels for gemini-api/gemini-local/claude-local/codex-local/fugu (status/kernel/p4-gemini-api-four-llm-transition-0625.md).

P5 repair: support/checkers/lib/case_runners.py added a deterministic in-process gemini-api preset-completion fixture; building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening keeps expected_frontier_kind: complete and passes for the full preset set including four-llm-standard-graph (status/kernel/p5-four-llm-gemini-api-fixture-repair-0625.md).

P10 permission matrix: effective_write = bool(write_scope) AND tool-policy:read-write-scoped AND adapter in _OBSERVED_WRITE_ADAPTER_REFS (agent_adapter.py:868-877). gemini-api is read+review only (never write); codex-local/claude-local/codex-fugu-local are observed-write; COO stays read-only (no read-write-scoped policy) (status/kernel/p10-agent-yaml-permission-matrix-reroute-0625.md).

NOTE: that smoke used adapter:gemini-local for the Gemini lane, whereas the current preset declares adapter:gemini-api — the gemini-api lane is not yet proven by an end-to-end smoke.

P7 evidence-root policy: ref-less DEFAULT_BUILDINGS_ROOT resolves under BRICK_EVIDENCE_HOME ($BRICK_HOME or ~/.brick); declared project_ref:'project:brick-protocol' resolves to the active checkout's project/brick-protocol/buildings; ambiguous project_ref + explicit output_root rejects (status/kernel/p7-evidence-root-location-policy-0625.md).

P9 diet: a selector guide (status/kernel/checker-profile-diet-selector-guide-0625.md) buckets profiles into quick/core, dogfood/behavioral, live-heavy/provider, oversized/history so operators avoid --all; the six-family classification + module-cleanup angle (god modules, write-scope conflicts on core.yaml/module_registry.yaml/case_runners.py) is the requested P9 followup scope (p9-checker-module-diet-followup-0625/work/task.md).

Observed count drift: support/checkers/profiles/ currently holds 24 profile YAMLs, while setup.md and AGENTS.md state '13 profiles'.

(All proposals are boundary suggestions only, not Movement or route decisions.)
